body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

**agent_supports: yes.** Aider 0.86.2 enforces a hard stop when a token/cost budget is hit mid-turn and resolves the turn cleanly. The cap surfaces through litellm's exception classification in `aider/exceptions.py`:

- `RateLimitError` (HTTP 429 / provider quota) is `retry: True` with the description "The API provider has rate limited you. Try again later or check your quotas."
- `BudgetExceededError` is also `retry: True` (litellm's own budget enforcement).
- The non-retryable insufficient-credits case (`APIError` with `"code":402`) returns `retry: False`, "Insufficient credits with the API provider."

The send loop in `coders/base_coder.py` (the `while True` around `self.send(...)`) doubles `retry_delay` (start 0.125s) on each retryable error; once `retry_delay > RETRY_TIMEOUT` (60s, `aider/models.py`) it sets `should_retry = False`, writes the error via `io.tool_warning(err_msg)` + `io.tool_error(description)`, and `break`s — ending the turn. The non-retryable cases break immediately. Either way the turn terminates and aider returns to its idle prompt; it does not silently re-enter `working`. There is no separate aider-native "subscription cost cap" feature — the budget is enforced provider-side (429) or by litellm (`BudgetExceededError`); aider's only knob is `RETRY_TIMEOUT` governing how long it waits before surfacing the cap.

**irrlicht_observes: partial.** The aider adapter source is `FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md}` with a markdown `RawParser` (`agent.go`). `io.tool_error`/`tool_warning` both route through `io._tool_message`, which calls `append_chat_history(..., blockquote=True)` — so the surfaced cap lands in the transcript as a `> ...Error...` blockquote line. irrlicht's parser matches this with `errorRE` (`^>\s*\S*(?:Error|Exception)(?:[: ]|$)`) and, while `turnOpen`, calls `flushErrorTurn` → emits `turn_done` with the error as `AssistantText`, flipping `LastEventType` so the state classifier transitions `working → ready`. This is the same #262 path the matrix note cites ("LLM error blockquote treated as turn_done; quota errors land in same path").

The scenario's primary User-observable signal — state reaches `ready` — is therefore observed. It is `partial` rather than `yes` because:

1. The capped model call never emits a `> Tokens: …` line, so `closeModelCall` never runs: no per-turn token/cost Contribution is reported for the budget-capped turn (only the error text). The budget cap is observed as a generic error-terminated turn, not a distinct budget/quota event.
2. The second half of the signal — "subsequent user prompts stay `ready` until the budget resets" — only holds in aider's non-interactive `--yes-always`/headless flow the adapter targets; a fresh user `####` prompt would re-open a turn and re-attempt, so the budget cap is not retained as state by the adapter.

## What would flip irrlicht_observes to yes

A budget/quota-specific event kind distinguishing a cost-cap turn-end from a generic LLM error (today both collapse to `turn_done` via `flushErrorTurn`), plus carrying the last-known usage on the capped turn.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: the budget cap reaches `ready` via the generic LLM-error blockquote path (#262), so irrlicht cannot tell a quota/budget cap apart from any other turn-aborting provider error.

metric-drift-downstream: the capped model call emits no `> Tokens:` line, so no per-turn token/cost Contribution is recorded for the budget-capped turn — only the error text is surfaced as AssistantText.

Aider retries a 429/RateLimitError with exponential backoff up to RETRY_TIMEOUT (60s) before surfacing it; during retries the session legitimately stays `working`, and only flips to `ready` once retries are exhausted.

sources.note: Source is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md} with a markdown RawParser
errorRE matches `> ...Error...` blockquotes; flushErrorTurn emits turn_done → working→ready (the #262 path)
aider 0.86.2: RateLimitError retry=True 'check your quotas'; BudgetExceededError retry=True; APIError+402 'Insufficient credits' retry=False
send loop: exponential retry_delay, when > RETRY_TIMEOUT should_retry=False → tool_warning+tool_error then break (turn ends)
tool_error/tool_warning → _tool_message → append_chat_history(blockquote=True): the error lands in .aider.chat.history.md as a `> ` line
RETRY_TIMEOUT = 60 — the retry ceiling before a 429/quota is surfaced
headless_mode: true (scenario's only `requires`); cost_tracking: false — aider has no native cost-cap feature, budget is provider/litellm-enforced
